Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall dimensions of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important Cultural Property) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a platform for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated artifacts were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}. Both sledges and the lever were designated as National Important other_designation in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | Name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}} Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage Designation = Two tombs designated as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall dimensions of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important Cultural Property) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a platform for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated artifacts were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}. Both sledges and the lever were designated as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)}}

* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (other_designation Online)

* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}} Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage Designation = Two tombs designated as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall dimensions of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important Cultural Property) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a platform for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated artifacts were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}. Both sledges and the lever were designated as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (other_designation Online)

Including the moat, the overall dimensions of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important Cultural Property) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a platform for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated artifacts were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}. Both sledges and the lever were designated as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}} Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Buried Figure = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage Designation = Two tombs designated as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National other_designation of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall dimensions of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important Cultural Property) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a platform for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated artifacts were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}. Both sledges and the lever were designated as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)}}

* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}} Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Buried Figure = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage Designation = Two tombs other_designation1 as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National {{ill|other_other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are other_designation1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National other_other_designation of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important Cultural Property) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}. Both sledges and the lever were other_designation1 as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (other_other_designation Online)

The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important Cultural Property) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}. Both sledges and the lever were other_designation1 as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.

* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}} Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Buried Figure = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage Designation = Two tombs other_designation1 as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are other_designation1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National other_designation of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important Cultural Property) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}. Both sledges and the lever were other_designation1 as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (other_designation Online)

== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}} Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Buried Figure = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage Designation = Two tombs other_designation1 as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are other_designation1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National other_designation of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important Cultural Property) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}. Both sledges and the lever were other_designation1 as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)}}

* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (other_designation Online)

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}} Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Buried Figure = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage Designation = Two tombs other_designation1 as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are other_designation1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National other_designation of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important Cultural Property) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}. Both sledges and the lever were other_designation1 as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (other_designation Online)

The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}} Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage Designation = Two tombs other_designation1 as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are other_designation1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National other_designation of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important Cultural Property) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}. Both sledges and the lever were other_designation1 as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}} Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage Designation = Two tombs other_designation1 as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are other_designation1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}. Both sledges and the lever were other_designation1 as National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)}}

Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498.== See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)}}
