Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|12=draft|12=draft}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|12=draft|12=draft}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=draft|12=draft}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|12=draft|12=draft}}

* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online)
of Japan]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft|12=draft}} Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|12=draft|12=draft}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|12=draft|12=draft}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=draft|12=draft}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|12=draft|12=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online)
of Japan]]

The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|12=draft|12=draft}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|12=draft|12=draft}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=draft|12=draft}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|12=draft|12=draft}}

of Japan]]

{{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft|12=draft}} Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|12=draft|12=draft}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|12=draft|12=draft}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=draft|12=draft}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft|12=draft}} Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|12=draft|12=draft}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|12=draft|12=draft}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=draft|12=draft}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|12=draft|12=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online)
of Japan]]

{{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft|12=draft}} Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|12=draft|12=draft}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|12=draft|12=draft}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=draft|12=draft}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|12=draft|12=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}

== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: 21 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:三ツ塚古墳 up to rev 91531512
revision.text:
<!--{{日本の古墳 |名称=三ツ塚古墳 |画像= |所在地=大阪府藤井寺市道明寺6丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|scale:}} |形状=方墳 |規模=一辺35m-50m |築造年代= 5世紀-7世紀 |埋葬施設=不明 |被葬者= |出土品=修羅 |史跡指定=2基は宮内庁指定仲姫命陵陪塚 |特記事項= }}--> [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|助太山古墳（国の[[史跡]]）}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|八島塚古墳（[[仲ツ山古墳|仲津山陵]]飛地ろ号）}}]] '''三ツ塚古墳'''（みつづかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]道明寺にある[[方墳]]3基（'''助太山古墳'''・'''中山塚古墳'''・'''八島塚古墳'''）の総称。中山塚古墳・八島塚古墳は[[宮内庁]]により[[仲姫命]][[仲ツ山古墳|仲津山陵]]の[[陪塚|陪冢]]に治定され、助太山古墳は国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
八島塚古墳と中山塚古墳の間の周濠から[[修羅 (そり)|修羅]]と呼ばれる木製のソリが出土したことで有名。なお、この3基の古墳の築造時期については議論がある。
== 概要== [[仲ツ山古墳]]の南東の周濠外堤に接して東西に並んで存在している、3基の方墳で、八島塚古墳と中山塚古墳は一辺50メートルの方墳であり、もう1基の助太山古墳は一辺35メートルの方墳である。助太山古墳は他の2基の3分の2の平面企画を持つようで3古墳とも同時に築造されたと考えられる。周濠の幅は助太山古墳の北側と西側のみ10メートルでその他の3墳を取り巻く部分では15メートルである。また、八島塚古墳と中山塚古墳の間は20メートル、中山塚古墳と助太山古墳との間は15メートルである。濠を含めた3墳の総規模は東西195メートル、南北80メートルである。一つの周濠内に3基の古墳が並んで存在しているわけである。
==修羅の出土== [[ファイル:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280px|right|{{center|[[修羅 (そり)|大修羅]]（国の[[重要文化財]]）}}{{small|[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|小修羅（国の重要文化財）}}{{small|[[藤井寺市立図書館]]展示。}}]] 1978年春、八島塚古墳と中山塚古墳の間の周濠底から大小2つの[[修羅 (そり)|修羅]]と呼ばれる木製の[[そり|ソリ]]が出土した。これらは、[[アカガシ]]属に属する自然木の、二股に枝分かれする部分を用材としている。大形のものは、全長8.8メートル、頭部先端を尖り気味に上方にそらせ、先端から1メートルのところに幅23センチ、高さ17センチの引き綱を通す「えつり穴」を穿ち、約2.2メートルのところから上部を平坦に加工し、荷にのせる部分を形成している。2本の足の側縁には各6ヶ所に斜め穴をあけ、荷綱を通したり、引き綱をとめる施設としている。小形の修羅は大形の修羅の南端に一部かさなる状態で出土している。全長2.9メートルを測り、先端から25センチのところに主綱を通す横穴をあけ、上面が平坦面を成すのは大形のものと同様である。また、これらの修羅を動かすのに用いられたと思われる梃子棒（テコぼう）も出土している。修羅の使用目的については付近の古墳の石棺や石室の大形石材の運搬に使用されたものと推定される。時期については、共判遺物が知られていないので断定できないが、周濠埋土が6世紀のものであることから、修羅も同時期と考えられるが、他の類例から7世紀頃とする見解がある。
その後、出土した大小2つの修羅と梃子棒は15年かけて、保存処理がなされた。現在、大形の修羅と梃子棒は[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]に保管展示されており、小形の修羅の方は[[藤井寺市立図書館]]に保管展示されている。2006年6月に大小の修羅と梃子棒は国の重要文化財に指定されている。
==築造時期に関する議論== 三ツ塚古墳の築造時期については、従来から[[埴輪]]が採集され、助太山古墳墳頂部には石材が露呈しており、これが[[竪穴式石室]]の蓋石と考えられ、すぐそばに存在する5世紀前半の仲ツ山古墳（伝仲姫命陵）の[[陪塚]]と理解されてきた。また[[宮内庁]]も、八島塚古墳と中山塚古墳の2基は仲姫命陵の陪塚に指定し、管理している。しかしながら、仲津山古墳が主軸を南西から北東に向けるのに対し、三ツ塚古墳が東西に並ぶことからそれを疑問視し、最近では7世紀の築造とする説もある。その根拠は軸方向が異なる事以外に、従来、竪穴式石室の蓋石とされた石材が、平坦面四方に面取り加工を施し、[[終末期古墳]]に酷似している。また、石材もこの付近の終末期古墳に使用されている[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]の牡丹洞産凝灰岩であることなどである。
==参考文献== *「三ツ塚古墳」『藤井寺市史第三巻 資料編一』 藤井寺市史編さん委員会 代表 秋山日出雄 1986年 298頁-300頁 *「修羅」『藤井寺市史第三巻 資料編一』 藤井寺市史編さん委員会 代表 秋山日出雄 1986年 497頁-498頁
==関連項目 == *[[古市古墳群]] *[[仲津山古墳]] *[[修羅 (そり)]] ==外部リンク== *[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html 助太山古墳]（藤井寺市ホームページ） *[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 修羅/梃子棒/大阪府三ツ塚古墳出土]（文化遺産オンライン）
{{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=助太山古墳 |1-1=古市古墳群 |1-2=大阪府の古墳 |1-3=藤井寺市の歴史 |1-4=方墳 |1-5=大阪府にある国指定の史跡 |redirect2=中山塚古墳 |2-1=古市古墳群 |2-2=大阪府の古墳 |2-3=藤井寺市の歴史 |2-4=方墳 |2-5=陵墓陪冢 |redirect3=八島塚古墳 (笠岡市) |3-1=古市古墳群 |3-2=大阪府の古墳 |3-3=藤井寺市の歴史 |3-4=方墳 |3-5=陵墓陪冢 }} {{デフォルトソート:みつつかこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[category:大阪府の重要文化財]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}} Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft}} Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft}} | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft}}})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|qq=draft}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft}}{{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft}}, Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|qq=draft}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|qq=draft}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|qq=draft}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|qq=draft}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft}} in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|qq=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}
{{translated page|ja|三ツ塚古墳|version=91531512|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.

== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsu Asuka Museum|qq=}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}. == References ==

Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}) Exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsu Asuka Museum|qq=}}.]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sledges|qq=}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsu Asuka Museum|qq=}}, while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}

Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]] Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sledges|qq=}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}

== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sledges|qq=}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}
