{{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]] Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sledges|qq=}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}

{{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]] Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sledges|qq=}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}
{{translated page|ja|三ツ塚古墳|version=91531512|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sledges|qq=}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]] Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sledges|qq=}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.

* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}

* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==

{{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]] Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sledges|qq=}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}

The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. == Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sledges|qq=}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}

Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.

The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. {{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]] Dōmyōji 6-Chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|60|N|135|61|05|E|map_size:}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] | notes = }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sledges|qq=}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}

Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.95|N|136.018056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] | notes = | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076}} }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sledges|qq=}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}

{{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.95|N|136.018056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] | notes = | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076}} }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622754}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sledges|qq=|1=Shura (sled)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386349}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622754}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}

While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==

{{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.95|N|136.018056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] | notes = | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}} }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622754}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sledges|1=Shura (sled)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386349}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622754}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.

* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}

* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}

== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622754}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sledges|1=Shura (sled)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386349}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622754}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}

{{infobox ancient site | name = Mitsuzuka Kofun | image = | location = {{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.95|N|136.018056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Type = Square Kofun (方墳) | area = 35m–50m per side | built = 5th–7th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Sledges (修羅) | Heritage designation1 = Two tombs Historic site as Mausoleum satellite tombs by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] | notes = | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}} }} [[File:Suketayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Suketayama Kofun (National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]})}}]] [[File:Yashimazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Yashimazuka Kofun ({{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} satellite tomb)}}]] '''Mitsuzuka Kofun''' (三ツ塚古墳) refers to three square Kofuns—'''Suketayama Kofun''', '''Nakayamazuka Kofun''', and '''Yashimazuka Kofun'''—located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Dōmyōji. Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun are Historic site by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as satellite tombs (陪塚) of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, while Suketayama Kofun is a National Historic Site of Japan.
Mitsuzuka Kofun is notable for the discovery of sledges (修羅) from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. Debate surrounds the construction period of the tombs.
== Overview ==
The Mitsuzuka Kofun consists of three square tombs situated southeast of {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}, along the outer embankment of its moat. Nakayamazuka Kofun and Yashimazuka Kofun each measure 50m per side, while Suketayama Kofun measures 35m per side, suggesting the three tombs were constructed simultaneously. The moat surrounding Suketayama Kofun is 10m wide on its northern and western sides, while the moat surrounding the other two tombs is 15m wide. The space between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun is 20m, and the space between Nakayamazuka and Suketayama Kofun is 15m. Including the moat, the overall area of the three tombs are approximately 195m east-to-west and 80m north-to-south.
== Discovery of the Sledges == [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 修羅.JPG|thumb|280x280px|Large sledge (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].]] [[File:三ツ塚古墳出土 小修羅.JPG|thumb|{{center|Small sledge (National Important designation1) Exhibited at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622754}}.}}]] In the spring of 1978, two wooden sledges, commonly known as {{ill|Sledges|ja|修羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sledges|1=Shura (sled)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386349}}, were excavated from the moat between Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun. These sledges were crafted from natural wood of the genus {{ill|Quercus|ja|アカガシ属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, utilizing bifurcated branches as the base material.
The larger sledge measures 8.8m in length. Its front is slightly pointed and curved upward, with a 23cm-wide, 17cm-high hole for pulling ropes located 1m from the tip. The upper portion is flattened to create a plattype for carrying loads, and six diagonal holes along each side are used to secure ropes. The smaller sledge, partially overlapping the larger one upon excavation, measures 2.9m in length. Like the larger sledge, it has a flattened upper surface and a hole for pulling ropes 25cm from its tip.
Levers believed to have been used to move the sledges were also discovered. The sledges are thought to have been used to transport large stones for coffins or chambers in nearby tombs. While no associated burial goods were found to confirm their date, the moat’s sediment suggests they date to the 6th century. However, some researchers propose they may be from the 7th century based on similarities to other sledges.
The sledges underwent 15 years of preservation treatment. Today, the larger sledge and the lever are exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while the smaller sledge is displayed at the {{ill|Fujiidera City Library|ja|藤井寺市立図書館|1=Fujidera Municipal Library|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera City Library|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Municipal Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622754}}. Both sledges and the lever were Historic site as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in June 2006.
== Debate on built ==
Traditionally, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs have been considered satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun, dated to the early 5th century, due to the collection of haniwa fragments and the exposed stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun believed to be part of a vertical stone chamber. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] manages Nakayamazuka and Yashimazuka Kofun as satellite tombs of Nakatsuyama Kofun.
However, some researchers question this interpretation due to differences in axis alignment. While Nakatsuyama Kofun is oriented southwest to northeast, the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs are aligned east to west. Furthermore, the stone slabs at Suketayama Kofun exhibit chamfering on all sides, resembling stone materials used in terminal-phase tombs. The use of tuff from [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in the Mitsuzuka Kofun tombs further supports a 7th-century construction date.
== References ==
* "Mitsuzuka Kofun" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 298–300. * "Sledges" in *Fujiidera City History, Volume III, Materials I*, edited by the Fujiidera City History Compilation Committee, 1986, pp. 497–498. == See Also ==
* [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}} * {{ill|Shura (sled)|ja|修羅 (そり)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shura (sled)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1031,96,139.html Suketayama Kofun] (Fujiidera City Official Website) * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/214159 Sledges/Levers from Mitsuzuka Kofun] (Historic site Online) {{Kofun navbar}}
